values.title: Professional Tools
values.description: CNC routers, laser cutters, 3D printers, welders, and full woodworking and metalworking shops.

values.title: Expert-Led Workshops
values.description: Weekly classes from safety certifications to advanced techniques taught by industry professionals.

values.title: Collaborative Community
values.description: 500+ makers sharing knowledge, tools, and inspiration across disciplines.

values.title: Safety First
values.description: Comprehensive safety training, well-maintained equipment, and trained staff always on duty.

values.title: Open Access Hours
values.description: Extended access hours for certified members, including evenings and weekends.

values.title: Prototype to Product
values.description: Resources to take your idea from concept to prototype to small-batch production.

values.title: Forge Makerspace
values.hero_description: A community workshop with professional-grade tools, expert instruction, and the collaborative spirit to bring your ideas to life.
values.cta_description: Tour our 15,000 sq ft facility and see what you could create. First workshop is free for new members.

values.title: Laser Cutting 101
values.body:
Learn to operate our Epilog and Glowforge laser cutters. Cover material selection, file preparation, safety protocols, and your first cut. Leave with a completed project.

You Will Learn

  • Machine operation and safety
  • Vector file preparation
  • Material settings for wood, acrylic, and leather
  • Engraving vs cutting techniques

values.title: Intro to Woodworking
values.body: A comprehensive introduction to the woodshop. Learn to use the table saw, miter saw, drill press, and hand tools safely. Build a beautiful cutting board to take home.

values.title: 3D Printing Masterclass
values.body: Go beyond basic 3D printing. Learn advanced slicer settings, multi-material printing, support strategies, and post-processing techniques for professional results.

values.title: MIG Welding Basics
values.body: Get started with MIG welding. Learn setup, safety, bead running, and basic joints. Complete a small welded project by the end of class.

values.title: Laser Cutters
values.body:
Two Epilog Fusion Pro 48 and one Glowforge Pro. Cut and engrave wood, acrylic, leather, fabric, and more with precision up to 0.001 inches.

Specifications

  • Epilog Fusion Pro 48 — 60W CO2, 48" x 36" bed
  • Glowforge Pro — 45W, camera alignment, passthrough slot

values.title: 3D Printers
values.body:
Eight 3D printers covering FDM, resin, and multi-material printing. From rapid prototyping to production-quality parts.

Available Machines

  • Prusa MK4 (x4) — PLA, PETG, ABS
  • Bambu Lab X1 Carbon (x2) — Multi-material
  • Formlabs Form 3+ (x2) — Resin SLA

values.title: Woodworking Shop
values.body:
Fully equipped woodshop with professional-grade tools for everything from fine furniture to large construction projects.

Available Tools

  • SawStop table saw
  • 12" Dewalt miter saw
  • Drill press, band saw, scroll saw
  • Router table and hand routers
  • Complete hand tool collection

values.title: Metalworking Shop
values.body:
Complete metalworking area with welding stations, plasma cutter, metal lathe, and sheet metal tools.

Available Equipment

  • Lincoln MIG welders (x3)
  • TIG welder
  • Hypertherm plasma cutter
  • South Bend metal lathe
  • Vertical mill

values.title: Spring Open House
values.body: Tour the entire facility, try out equipment with trained guides, and see what our members have been building. Free admission, refreshments provided.

values.title: 48-Hour Build-a-thon
values.body: Teams of 3-5 compete to design and build a functional prototype in 48 hours. Prizes awarded for creativity, execution, and impact. Open to all skill levels.

values.title: Community Repair Cafe
values.body: Bring broken household items and our volunteer fixers will help you repair them for free. Electronics, textiles, furniture, and more. Reduce waste, learn skills.

values.title: About Forge Makerspace
values.body:
Forge Makerspace was founded in 2017 by a group of makers, engineers, and artists who believed that access to professional tools and collaborative community could transform ideas into reality.

Our Mission

To democratize access to fabrication tools and knowledge, empowering people to design, prototype, and build.

The Space

15,000 sq ft purpose-built facility with dedicated areas for woodworking, metalworking, digital fabrication, electronics, and textiles.

values.title: FAQ
values.body:
Do I need experience?

Not at all. We offer beginner workshops for every tool area. Safety certification is required before using certain equipment.

What does membership include?

Access to the facility during open hours, storage space, member pricing on workshops, and community events.

Can I bring my own materials?

Yes! We also sell common materials like plywood, acrylic sheets, and filament at competitive prices.

Is there storage for projects?

All members get a storage shelf. Larger project storage is available for an additional fee.
